title: Why Eval++ Is the Next Great Compute Primitive — Sunil Pai & Matt Carrie, Cloudflare
summary: Cloudflare's Durable Objects provide addressable, persistent, stateful compute units suitable for AI agents, with latency low enough (15ms) to meet animation frame budgets. Dynamic Workers enable running LLM-generated code in sandboxed isolates with explicit capability grants, positioning Eval++ as a compute primitive for agent execution and code generation workflows.
writeup: Sunil Pai and Matt Carrie present Cloudflare's case for "Eval++" as a compute primitive for AI agents: running LLM-generated code in sandboxed isolates through Dynamic Workers, with explicit capability grants controlling what that code can access. Durable Objects supply the addressable, persistent, stateful compute units agents need, with latency low enough (15ms) to fit animation frame budgets. The framing matters because agent execution and code generation increasingly require running untrusted, model-produced code safely while preserving state across calls. Concrete points include the pairing of stateful compute with sandboxed execution, plus streaming and MCP as part of the workflow.
